dramatic_function: Introduce Kannaki as remembered catastrophe and establish that the story is received and transmitted
Establish royal city, maritime abundance and the scale of the world that will be lost
Introduce Kannaki and Kovalan through wealth, family expectation and adult casting rather than romantic myth alone
Establish artistic labour, public mastery, patronage and the beginning of Kovalan's attachment
Show intimacy, status and depletion of wealth without reducing Madhavi to a moral cause
Use performance and misreading to turn artistic expression into relational rupture
Make apology, anger, remaining property and the decision to leave dramatically legible

working_scene_title: The anklet wrapped for the road
dramatic_function: Transform the anklet from household object into survival property and bind the couple to a final shared risk
Strip away urban protection and reveal ecological, religious and social knowledge
Establish the capital as prosperous, inhabited and institutionally confident before failure
Place Kannaki in temporary protection while Kovalan enters an unfamiliar market alone
Activate prior theft, specialist recognition and deliberate false accusation
Show accusation becoming royal violence through an untested chain of authority
Complete the wrongful killing without implying moral punishment for earlier failures
Turn private restraint into embodied grief and deliberate movement toward public truth
Make the city-circling movement a public gathering of testimony rather than a transition montage
Use material evidence to overturn royal certainty and expose failed government
Separate recognition, responsibility, grief and collapse from the later destruction of the city
Stage Kannaki's vow and the burning as sacred event and inhabited civic catastrophe
Hold former-birth causation beside present human and governmental responsibility
Let Kannaki state her own catastrophe before the hill people interpret what they witness
Show collective music, flowers, smoke and protective boundary as active deification
Shift from local witness to royal memory-making and interstate scale
Conclude with the labour, politics and ritual through which the story becomes institution
